disambiguated.context: I've spent weeks conducting a survey targeting speakers of different varieties of English, and the last two calls I had to make today were to a judge and a construction worker. I had a great conversation with the construction worker, who was very helpful, but I had so much trouble understanding what the judge was saying that I just gave up after about two minutes.
disambiguated.question: Who speaks proper English?

stages.reason: The context states difficulty understanding the judge, but doesn't define 'proper English' or definitively state who speaks it. The helpfulness of the construction worker doesn't equate to 'proper' speech.

final.reason: Insufficient evidence. The context only indicates difficulty understanding the judge, and helpfulness of the construction worker. It doesn't define 'proper English' or definitively identify who speaks it.
